content.subtitle: Use
items: Situations or states that began in the past and continue now
Actions that happened at some point in the past when the exact time isn't given
Past actions whose results affect the present moment

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Mr Torres **has taught** at this school for over ten years.
I **have never tried** sushi — this will be the first time!
We can't go out — Mum **has lost** her keys.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: We don't use the present perfect simple when we want to say **when** something happened in the past. We use the past simple: ✓ I **did** my homework **last night**.
We don't use the past simple when we want to show that something happened before now or is still important now. We use the present perfect simple: ✓ I've **finished**! Can I go home now?
Some verbs have irregular past participle forms.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Activities that have been going on up to the present or have just stopped

content.subtitle: Examples
items: I **have been waiting** here for half an hour — where have you been?
She looks exhausted because she **has been running**.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: The present perfect continuous is often used with the following words:
rows: I've been learning English **for** over three years.
I've **just** been reading the school newspaper.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: The present perfect simple often emphasises the result of an action: ✓ She's **written** an article for the school newspaper. (= She's finished it.)

The present perfect continuous often emphasises the action, and the time spent on the action, rather than the result: ✓ She's **been writing** an article for the school newspaper. (= She's started, but she hasn't finished it yet.)

sections.title: A: Present perfect simple
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct present perfect simple form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: Emily ...... (live) in this town all her life.
The teacher ...... (give) us too much homework!
They ...... (not / choose) a name for the puppy yet.
We ...... to the zoo last weekend.
It's the first time ...... this game.
...... to Paris when you were in France?

sections.title: C: Write sentences
sections.instructions: Look at the picture and use the prompts to write sentences. Use the correct form of the present perfect simple.

items.answer: Dad has already bought the popcorn.
The children have just sat down.

items.sentence: Tom / already / turn off / his phone
items.answer: Tom has already turned off his phone.

items.sentence: someone / drop / a ticket / on the floor
items.answer: Someone has dropped a ticket on the floor.

items.sentence: they / not / pick it up / yet
items.answer: They haven't picked it up yet.

sections.title: D: Present perfect continuous
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct present perfect continuous form of the verbs in brackets. Use short forms where possible.
sections.passage:
Lucy: Hi Ben! You look tired. What (1) ...... (you / do)?
Ben: Hey Lucy. Well, (2) ...... (I / revise) for my maths test.
Lucy: Really? (3) ...... (you / study) all day?
Ben: Pretty much. (4) ...... (I / sit) in the library since this morning and (5) ...... (I / not / take) any breaks.
Lucy: That sounds awful!
Ben: Yeah, but (6) ...... (I / work) with Alex, so it's been a bit easier.
Lucy: Well, my sister and (7) ...... (I / clean) the house all afternoon.
Ben: Fun!
Lucy: Actually, yes! And (8) ...... (we / also / prepare) dinner for tonight.
Ben: Nice. What are you making?
Lucy: Pizza! We can't decide on the toppings, so (9) ...... (we / look) at recipes online.
Ben: Sounds great. By the way, (10) ...... (I / think) about joining the school basketball team.

sections.title: E: Circle the correct word or phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: I think I've **seen / been seeing** that actor before.
She hasn't **finished / been finishing** her project yet.
You've **studied / been studying** for hours. Take a break!
Dad has already **washed / been washing** the car.
I've **read / been reading** this book all week, but I still haven't finished it.
Have you **played / been playing** football since this morning?

sections.title: F: Complete using the words in the box
sections.instructions: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: She's been learning Spanish ...... three years.
I've ...... spoken to Mia on the phone.
